items.headline: Major commercial project approved on West San Carlos Street
items.summary: A $696,570 commercial/industrial development is underway at 1300 W San Carlos St, marking one of the larger commercial permits in this batch. This represents new business investment in San José's industrial corridor.

items.headline: Cupertino launches business license amnesty program for shopping centers
items.summary: The city is offering an amnesty program to help previously unlicensed businesses at local shopping centers get into compliance with licensing requirements. This could affect local businesses and the city's tax revenue from retail operations.

items.headline: City announces economic incentives for retail and small businesses
items.summary: Cupertino is introducing new incentive programs aimed at attracting and supporting retail shops, small businesses, and startups in the area. The move signals the city's focus on maintaining a vibrant local business community amid broader economic pressures.

items.headline: City applies for federal housing and development grants
items.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission reviewed Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) funding applicants on March 25. This federal funding supports affordable housing and community development projects in Sunnyvale.

items.headline: City updates zoning and building rules to boost retail business
items.summary: Palo Alto is amending zoning and building regulations to implement retail vitality policies from its Comprehensive Plan. This could affect where retail businesses can locate and how they operate throughout the city.

items.headline: San Antonio Road area plan undergoing transportation study
items.summary: The Planning Commission is reviewing land use and transportation alternatives for the San Antonio Road area. This study could lead to changes in how that corridor is developed and how traffic flows through it.

items.headline: Campbell updates beekeeping rules with new ordinance
items.summary: The City Council adopted an updated beekeeping ordinance on March 24, clarifying regulations for residents who keep bees. This signals the city's stance on urban beekeeping and what backyard beekeepers can and cannot do.

items.headline: Senate Bill 79 transit development rules spark council conflicts
items.summary: Three council members recused themselves from a public hearing on Senate Bill 79 (Transit-Oriented Development) due to property conflicts within the affected half-mile zone around transit, requiring the council to invoke a rule of necessity to proceed. This affects what types of housing and development can happen near Campbell transit corridors.
